devanagari: वसुदेवदेवकी सान्त्वनम् उग्रसेनस्य राज्याभिषेकः रामकृष्णयोरुपनयनं विद्याध्ययनं गुरुर्मृतपुत्रस्यानयनं च । अथ पञ्चचत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः । श्र...
english_devnagari: vasudevadevakī sāntvanam ugrasenasya rājyābhiṣekaḥ rāmakṛṣṇayorupanayanaṃ vidyādhyayanaṃ gururmṛtaputrasyānayanaṃ caatha pañcacatvāriṃśo'dhyāyaḥśrīśuka uvācapitarāvupalabdhārthau viditvā puruṣottamaḥmā bhūditi nijāṃ māyāṃ tatāna janamohinīm
translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
Noticing that his parents, Vasudeva and Devakī, had realized the truth (of their Divine Nature) prematurely (before enjoying the filial bliss in loving them as their children), the Supreme Person, Kṛṣṇa, cast over them his divine Māyā (the spell of his deluding faculty) which infatuates the world, so that this realization (of his God-hood) should not continue (as it would not give scope to their parental love and affect their affectionate filial behaviour).

devanagari: उवाच पितरावेत्य साग्रजः सात्वर्षभः । प्रश्रयावनतः प्रीणन्नम्ब तातेति सादरम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāca pitarāvetya sāgrajaḥ sātvarṣabhaḥpraśrayāvanataḥ prīṇannamba tāteti sādaram
translation: Kṛṣṇa, the bull (prominent person) among the Sātvatas, approached along with his elder brother to his parents. He bowed down with great modesty, and respectfully addressed them: “Oh Mother” and “Oh father.”

devanagari: नास्मत्तो युवयोस्तात नित्योत्कण्ठितयोरपि । बाल्यपौगण्डकैशोराः पुत्राभ्यामभवन्क्वचित् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsmatto yuvayostāta nityotkaṇṭhitayorapibālyapaugaṇḍakaiśorāḥ putrābhyāmabhavankvacit
translation: “Father! Though both of you have been all along longing earnestly for it, we could not, at any time, give you the pleasure of witnessing the infancy, childhood and boyhood of your sons.

devanagari: न लब्धो दैवहतयोर्वासो नौ भवदन्तिके । यां बालाः पितृगेहस्था विन्दन्ते लालिता मुदम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: na labdho daivahatayorvāso nau bhavadantikeyāṃ bālāḥ pitṛgehasthā vindante lālitā mudam
translation: Unfortunate as we are, we had not the good luck of staying near you nor could we enjoy the bliss of being caressed by parents which children dwelling in their parent’s home experience.

devanagari: सर्वार्थसम्भवो देहो जनितः पोषितो यतः । न तयोर्याति निर्वेशं पित्रोर्मर्त्यः शतायुषा ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvārthasambhavo deho janitaḥ poṣito yataḥna tayoryāti nirveśaṃ pitrormartyaḥ śatāyuṣā
translation: It is not possible for a mortal being, even within the full span of human life of hundred years, to repay the obligations of parents who brought forth and nourished his body whereby all the four objects in life (like dharma, artha etc.) are achieved.

devanagari: यस्तयोरात्मजः कल्प आत्मना च धनेन च । वृत्तिं न दद्यात्तं प्रेत्य स्वमांसं खादयन्ति हि ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yastayorātmajaḥ kalpa ātmanā ca dhanena cavṛttiṃ na dadyāttaṃ pretya svamāṃsaṃ khādayanti hi
translation: If he (the son) be (physically and financially) competent but does not maintain them by rendering personal service or financial help, he is made to eat his own flesh (in hell) after his death, by servants of Yama.

devanagari: मातरं पितरं वृद्धं भार्यां साध्वीं सुतं शिशुम् । गुरुं विप्रं प्रपन्नं च कल्पोऽबिभ्रच्छ्वसन्मृतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: mātaraṃ pitaraṃ vṛddhaṃ bhāryāṃ sādhvīṃ sutaṃ śiśumguruṃ vipraṃ prapannaṃ ca kalpo'bibhracchvasanmṛtaḥ
translation: He who, in spite of his competence (to support) his old mother, father, chaste wife and infant son, his preceptor, (learned) Brāhmaṇa and one seeking his resort, is as good as dead though breathing.

devanagari: तन्नावकल्पयोः कंसान्नित्यमुद्विग्नचेतसोः । मोघमेते व्यतिक्रान्ता दिवसा वामनर्चतोः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tannāvakalpayoḥ kaṃsānnityamudvignacetasoḥmoghamete vyatikrāntā divasā vāmanarcatoḥ
translation: With our minds ever in terror of Kaṃsa and due to our inability to worship you both, all these days have been sheerly wasted away.

devanagari: तत्क्षन्तुमर्हथस्तात मातर्नौ परतन्त्रयोः । अकुर्वतोर्वां शुश्रूषां क्लिष्टयोर्दुर्हृदा भृशम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkṣantumarhathastāta mātarnau paratantrayoḥakurvatorvāṃ śuśrūṣāṃ kliṣṭayordurhṛdā bhṛśam
translation: It behoves you, Oh father and mother, to forgive us, since we were dependants and unable to render service to you, even though you were.mercilessly harassed by that wicked- hearted fellow Kaṃsa.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति मायामनुष्यस्य हरेर्विश्वात्मनो गिरा । मोहितावङ्कमारोप्य परिष्वज्यापतुर्मुदम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti māyāmanuṣyasya harerviśvātmano girāmohitāvaṅkamāropya pariṣvajyāpaturmudam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Fascinated by the charming words of Lord Hari who, though the soul of the universe, had assumed a human form through his Māyā Potency, they placed him on their lap, embraced him and felt greatly delighted.

devanagari: सिञ्चन्तावश्रुधाराभिः स्नेहपाशेन चावृतौ । न किञ्चिदूचतू राजन्बाष्पकण्ठौ विमोहितौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: siñcantāvaśrudhārābhiḥ snehapāśena cāvṛtauna kiñcidūcatū rājanbāṣpakaṇṭhau vimohitau
translation: Oh King! Bound with ties of (filial) affection, they bathed him profusely with their tears. Being bewildered and with their throats choked with tears and emotions, they could utter nothing.

devanagari: एवमाश्वास्य पितरौ भगवान्देवकीसुतः । मातामहं तूग्रसेनं यदूनामकरोन्नृपम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: evamāśvāsya pitarau bhagavāndevakīsutaḥmātāmahaṃ tūgrasenaṃ yadūnāmakaronnṛpam
translation: Having thus comforted his parents, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, (immediately) installed his maternal grandfather as the king of Yadus.

devanagari: आह चास्मान्महाराज प्रजाश्चाज्ञप्तुमर्हसि । ययातिशापाद्यदुभिर्नासितव्यं नृपासने ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: āha cāsmānmahārāja prajāścājñaptumarhasiyayātiśāpādyadubhirnāsitavyaṃ nṛpāsane
translation: He addressed him (Ugrasena): “Oh great king! May Your Majesty be pleased to command us (Yadus) as well as the subjects (of the Śūrasena country). Owing to an imprecation from Yayāti, Yadus are not entitled to occupy a throne.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Despite Yadu’s refusal to transfer his youth to his father for enjoying his mother (a serious moral problem), Yadu was given the southern part of his kingdom by his father (vide Supra 9.19.22). Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: ‘Though you belong to the Yadu clan, you will not incur any sin as you occupy the throne by my command.’

devanagari: मयि भृत्य उपासीने भवतो विबुधादयः । बलिं हरन्त्यवनताः किमुतान्ये नराधिपाः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: mayi bhṛtya upāsīne bhavato vibudhādayaḥbaliṃ harantyavanatāḥ kimutānye narādhipāḥ
translation: When I attend upon you as your servant, even gods (and other supernatural beings) will submissively offer worship to you. What then to speak of other chiefs of human beings? They will definitely do so.

devanagari: सर्वान्स्वान्ज्ञातिसम्बन्धान्दिग्भ्यः कंसभयाकुलान् । यदुवृष्ण्यन्धकमधु दाशार्हकुकुरादिकान् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvānsvānjñātisambandhāndigbhyaḥ kaṃsabhayākulānyaduvṛṣṇyandhakamadhu dāśārhakukurādikān
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Creator of the Universe, brought back his kith and kin—Yadus, Vṛṣṇis, Andhakas, Dāśārhas, Kukuras and others, who being overcome with the fear from Kaṃsa, had fled to different directions and had undergone suffering in foreign lands. He consoled them, providing them with sufficient wealth and wherewithals and rehabilitated them in their homes.

devanagari: सभाजितान्समाश्वास्य विदेशावासकर्शितान् । न्यवासयत्स्वगेहेषु वित्तैः सन्तर्प्य विश्वकृत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhājitānsamāśvāsya videśāvāsakarśitānnyavāsayatsvageheṣu vittaiḥ santarpya viśvakṛt
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Creator of the Universe, brought back his kith and kin—Yadus, Vṛṣṇis, Andhakas, Dāśārhas, Kukuras and others, who being overcome with the fear from Kaṃsa, had fled to different directions and had undergone suffering in foreign lands. He consoled them, providing them with sufficient wealth and wherewithals and rehabilitated them in their homes.

devanagari: कृष्णसङ्कर्षणभुजैर्गुप्ता लब्धमनोरथाः । गृहेषु रेमिरे सिद्धाः कृष्णरामगतज्वराः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasaṅkarṣaṇabhujairguptā labdhamanorathāḥgṛheṣu remire siddhāḥ kṛṣṇarāmagatajvarāḥ
translation: Protected by the arms of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma and with all their agonies removed by Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, they enjoyed themselves in their homes with all their desire accomplished.

devanagari: वीक्षन्तोऽहरहः प्रीता मुकुन्दवदनाम्बुजम् । नित्यं प्रमुदितं श्रीमत्सदयस्मितवीक्षणम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: vīkṣanto'harahaḥ prītā mukundavadanāmbujamnityaṃ pramuditaṃ śrīmatsadayasmitavīkṣaṇam
translation: (Having the opportunity of) seeing daily the lotus-like countenance of Kṛṣṇa graced with splendour and beauty and beaming with smiles and compassionate looks, all of them were delighted.

devanagari: तत्र प्रवयसोऽप्यासन्युवानोऽतिबलौजसः । पिबन्तोऽक्षैर्मुकुन्दस्य मुखाम्बुजसुधां मुहुः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra pravayaso'pyāsanyuvāno'tibalaujasaḥpibanto'kṣairmukundasya mukhāmbujasudhāṃ muhuḥ
translation: Even aged persons thereof, who could drink with their eyes the nectarine beauty of his lotus-face, became rejuvenated with abundant strength and energy.

devanagari: अथ नन्दं समसाद्य भगवान्देवकीसुतः । सङ्कर्षणश्च राजेन्द्र परिष्वज्येदमूचतुः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: atha nandaṃ samasādya bhagavāndevakīsutaḥsaṅkarṣaṇaśca rājendra pariṣvajyedamūcatuḥ
translation: Thereupon, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Son of Devakī, and Balarāma approached Nanda, and embracing him, spoke to him as follows, Oh King of kings.

devanagari: पितर्युवाभ्यां स्निग्धाभ्यां पोषितौ लालितौ भृशम् । पित्रोरभ्यधिका प्रीतिरात्मजेष्वात्मनोऽपि हि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: pitaryuvābhyāṃ snigdhābhyāṃ poṣitau lālitau bhṛśampitrorabhyadhikā prītirātmajeṣvātmano'pi hi
translation: “Father! Both of us have been extremely fondled and nourished by you both (Nanda and Yaśodā) who have been highly affectionate to us. Parents cherish more love towards their children than to their own selves.

devanagari: स पिता सा च जननी यौ पुष्णीतां स्वपुत्रवत् । शिशून्बन्धुभिरुत्सृष्टानकल्पैः पोषरक्षणे ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa pitā sā ca jananī yau puṣṇītāṃ svaputravatśiśūnbandhubhirutsṛṣṭānakalpaiḥ poṣarakṣaṇe
translation: He is the real father and she is the (de facto) mother who nourish and caress like their own sons, other babes who are abandoned by their relatives, due to their incapacity to feed and protect them.

devanagari: यात यूयं व्रजं तात वयं च स्नेहदुःखितान् । ज्ञातीन्वो द्रष्टुमेष्यामो विधाय सुहृदां सुखम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yāta yūyaṃ vrajaṃ tāta vayaṃ ca snehaduḥkhitānjñātīnvo draṣṭumeṣyāmo vidhāya suhṛdāṃ sukham
translation: Father! Now all of you return to your Vraja. After looking to the welfare and felicity of friends hereof, we shall come to visit you, our kith and kin who due to our separation from them are grieved through their love for us.”

devanagari: एवं सान्त्वय्य भगवान्नन्दं सव्रजमच्युतः । वासोऽलङ्कारकुप्याद्यैरर्हयामास सादरम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sāntvayya bhagavānnandaṃ savrajamacyutaḥvāso'laṅkārakupyādyairarhayāmāsa sādaram
translation: Having consoled Nanda along with the residents of Vraja in this way, the glorious Immutable Lord (Kṛṣṇa) respectfully worshipped them with garments, ornaments, utensils (of copper, brass etc. as milk-containers) and other gifts.

devanagari: इत्युक्तस्तौ परिष्वज्य नन्दः प्रणयविह्वलः । पूरयन्नश्रुभिर्नेत्रे सह गोपैर्व्रजं ययौ ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktastau pariṣvajya nandaḥ praṇayavihvalaḥpūrayannaśrubhirnetre saha gopairvrajaṃ yayau
translation: Nanda who was addressed thus (by Kṛṣṇa) was overwhelmed with filial love. With his eyes streaming with tears, he hugged them both and returned to Vraja along with Gopas.

devanagari: अथ शूरसुतो राजन्पुत्रयोः समकारयत् । पुरोधसा ब्राह्मणैश्च यथावद्द्विजसंस्कृतिम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: atha śūrasuto rājanputrayoḥ samakārayatpurodhasā brāhmaṇaiśca yathāvaddvijasaṃskṛtim
translation: Thereafter, Vasudeva, the son of Śūra caused as per Śāstric prescriptions, the ceremony of the investiture of the sacred thread[2] (essential for the status of the ‘twice-borns’) (of their sons) by his family priest Garga and other Brāhmaṇas.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
As the presence of mother is essential in the sacred thread ceremony, it is obvious that Balarāma’s mother, Rohiṇī was brought from Vraja—VT.

devanagari: तेभ्योऽदाद्दक्षिणा गावो रुक्ममालाः स्वलङ्कृताः । स्वलङ्कृतेभ्यः सम्पूज्य सवत्साः क्षौममालिनीः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyo'dāddakṣiṇā gāvo rukmamālāḥ svalaṅkṛtāḥsvalaṅkṛtebhyaḥ sampūjya savatsāḥ kṣaumamālinīḥ
translation: After adorning the Brāhmaṇas with ornaments, he worshipped them and gave as dakṣiṇā (fee for performing this ceremony) cows with their calves, decorated with gold chains and other ornaments and covered with silk-cloth.

devanagari: याः कृष्णरामजन्मर्क्षे मनोदत्ता महामतिः । ताश्चाददादनुस्मृत्य कंसेनाधर्मतो हृताः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yāḥ kṛṣṇarāmajanmarkṣe manodattā mahāmatiḥtāścādadādanusmṛtya kaṃsenādharmato hṛtāḥ
translation: The noble-minded Vasudeva recollected the cows which he donated mentally, according to the constellation that was in ascendance at the time of the birth of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa but (which he could not give, as they) were seized illegally and unrighteously by Kaṃsa. And he gave those cows also (which he intended to give).

devanagari: ततश्च लब्धसंस्कारौ द्विजत्वं प्राप्य सुव्रतौ । गर्गाद्यदुकुलाचार्याद्गायत्रं व्रतमास्थितौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca labdhasaṃskārau dvijatvaṃ prāpya suvrataugargādyadukulācāryādgāyatraṃ vratamāsthitau
translation: Thereupon, being consecrated (through the investiture of the sacred thread) and attaining the status of the ‘twice- borns’, those two staunch observers of vows adopted the vow of celibacy (lit. vow pertaining to the Gayatrī mantra) from the sage Garga, the family priest of Yadus.

devanagari: प्रभवौ सर्वविद्यानां सर्वज्ञौ जगदीश्वरौ । नान्यसिद्धामलं ज्ञानं गूहमानौ नरेहितैः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: prabhavau sarvavidyānāṃ sarvajñau jagadīśvaraunānyasiddhāmalaṃ jñānaṃ gūhamānau narehitaiḥ
translation: Though both of them were the source of all lores, and the omniscient Lords of the worlds, they concealed their unclouded spiritual knowledge which is in-born and not borrowed from others, by behaving like common men.

devanagari: अथो गुरुकुले वासमिच्छन्तावुपजग्मतुः । काश्यं सान्दीपनिं नाम ह्यवन्तिपुरवासिनम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: atho gurukule vāsamicchantāvupajagmatuḥkāśyaṃ sāndīpaniṃ nāma hyavantipuravāsinam
translation: Desirous of having a residence in a preceptor’s house for their education, they approached (and served Sāndīpani who was originally a resident of Kāśī[3] but was now a citizen of Avantī.

devanagari: यथोपसाद्य तौ दान्तौ गुरौ वृत्तिमनिन्दिताम् । ग्राहयन्तावुपेतौ स्म भक्त्या देवमिवादृतौ ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yathopasādya tau dāntau gurau vṛttimaninditāmgrāhayantāvupetau sma bhaktyā devamivādṛtau
translation: The self-controlled brothers formally approached him with due humility and respect, and set a model of faultless behaviour with the teacher, (for others to emulate). Both of them who were respectfully treated, served their teacher with devotion, as if he were a god.

devanagari: तयोर्द्विजवरस्तुष्टः शुद्धभावानुवृत्तिभिः । प्रोवाच वेदानखिलान् साङ्गोपनिषदो गुरुः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tayordvijavarastuṣṭaḥ śuddhabhāvānuvṛttibhiḥprovāca vedānakhilān sāṅgopaniṣado guruḥ
translation: Being highly pleased with their pure-hearted devoted services, the eminent Brāhmaṇa teacher, Sāndīpani taught them all the Vedas along with their Upaniṣads (the philosophical works) and the accessory branches (viz. Śikṣā—phonetics, chandas—prosody, Vyākaraṇa—grammar, Jyotiṣa—Astronomy, Kalpa—science of Vedic ritualism and Nirukta—etymology).

devanagari: सरहस्यं धनुर्वेदं धर्मान्न्यायपथांस्तथा । तथा चान्वीक्षिकीं विद्यां राजनीतिं च षड्विधाम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sarahasyaṃ dhanurvedaṃ dharmānnyāyapathāṃstathātathā cānvīkṣikīṃ vidyāṃ rājanītiṃ ca ṣaḍvidhām
translation: He taught them Military science (lit. the Veda pertaining to archery) along with the mystic formulae (mantras) of discharging and retracting astras (missiles), dharma-śāstra (Law incorporated into the codes of Manu etc.), Mīmāṃsā, Logic and political science along with its six branches[4] (viz. techniques regarding peace-treaties, warfare, expedition, encampment, spreading sedition and dis-array among enemies and consolidation of alliance).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and Padaratnāvalī quote the following verse enumerating these branches—
sandhiṃ ca vigrahaṃ caiva yānam āsanam eva ca /
dvaidhī-bhāvaṃ saṃśrayañ ca ṣaḍ-guṇāḥ kavayo viduḥ //

devanagari: सर्वं नरवरश्रेष्ठौ सर्वविद्याप्रवर्तकौ । सकृन्निगदमात्रेण तौ सञ्जगृहतुर्नृप ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvaṃ naravaraśreṣṭhau sarvavidyāpravartakausakṛnnigadamātreṇa tau sañjagṛhaturnṛpa
translation: Those two greatest among the great, who were themselves the progenitors of all lores mastered everything that the teachers uttered but once to them (while teaching), Oh protector of men!

devanagari: अहोरात्रैश्चतुःषष्ट्या संयत्तौ तावतीः कलाः* । गुरुदक्षिणयाचार्यं छन्दयामासतुर्नृप ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahorātraiścatuḥṣaṣṭyā saṃyattau tāvatīḥ kalāḥ*gurudakṣiṇayācāryaṃ chandayāmāsaturnṛpa
translation: Within a period of sixty-four days and nights, those brothers, with controlled minds, mastered as many (i.e. sixty-four) arts and crafts [see notes], and persuaded their preceptor to accept whatever he liked as his fee of teaching.

devanagari: द्विजस्तयोस्तं महिमानमद्भुतं । संलक्ष्य राजन्नतिमानुषीं मतिम् । सम्मन्त्र्य पत्न्या स महार्णवे मृतं । बालं प्रभासे वरयां बभूव ह ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: dvijastayostaṃ mahimānamadbhutaṃsaṃlakṣya rājannatimānuṣīṃ matimsammantrya patnyā sa mahārṇave mṛtaṃbālaṃ prabhāse varayāṃ babhūva ha
translation: Noticing their miraculous capacity and greatness and their super human intelligence, the Brāhmaṇa, after consultation with his wife, sought (the restoration to life of) his son who died in the ocean at Prabhāsa (paṭṭaṇa [paṭṭana?] in Saurāṣṭra), as his fee.

devanagari: तथेत्यथारुह्य महारथौ रथं । प्रभासमासाद्य दुरन्तविक्रमौ । वेलामुपव्रज्य निषीदतुः क्षणं । सिन्धुर्विदित्वार्हणमाहरत्तयोः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tathetyathāruhya mahārathau rathaṃprabhāsamāsādya durantavikramauvelāmupavrajya niṣīdatuḥ kṣaṇaṃsindhurviditvārhaṇamāharattayoḥ
translation: Complying with his request with the word, “So be it”, those two great heroes (who could single-handedly fight with ten thousand warriors) and were of unlimited prowess, then mounted their chariot, arrived at Prabhāsa and going to the beach thereof, sat there for a moment. Having come to know of the arrival of those divine personages, the god presiding over the ocean, worshipped them with valuable presents.

devanagari: तमाह भगवानाशु गुरुपुत्रः प्रदीयताम् । योऽसाविह त्वया ग्रस्तो बालको महतोर्मिणा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāha bhagavānāśu guruputraḥ pradīyatāmyo'sāviha tvayā grasto bālako mahatormiṇā
translation: The glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa spoke to him: “Let the son of my preceptor who was swallowed up by you in a big wave, be restored immediately.”

devanagari: श्रीसमुद्र उवाच । न चाहार्षमहं देव दैत्यः पञ्चजनो महान् । अन्तर्जलचरः कृष्ण शङ्खरूपधरोऽसुरः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīsamudra uvācana cāhārṣamahaṃ deva daityaḥ pañcajano mahānantarjalacaraḥ kṛṣṇa śaṅkharūpadharo'suraḥ
translation:
The Presiding Deity of the sea replied:
“Oh refulgent Lord Kṛṣṇa! I have never carried away that son of your preceptor. But there is a formidable demon called Pañcajana who moves through the waters. He is an Asura who has assumed the form of a conch As a matter of fact, the boy has been carried away by him.” Hearing that, the glorious Lord instantly plunged into the water, killed the demon (Pañcajana) but could not trace the child in his bowels.

devanagari: आस्ते तेनाहृतो नूनं तच्छ्रुत्वा सत्वरं प्रभुः । जलमाविश्य तं हत्वा नापश्यदुदरेऽर्भकम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: āste tenāhṛto nūnaṃ tacchrutvā satvaraṃ prabhuḥjalamāviśya taṃ hatvā nāpaśyadudare'rbhakam
translation:
The Presiding Deity of the sea replied:
“Oh refulgent Lord Kṛṣṇa! I have never carried away that son of your preceptor. But there is a formidable demon called Pañcajana who moves through the waters. He is an Asura who has assumed the form of a conch As a matter of fact, the boy has been carried away by him.” Hearing that, the glorious Lord instantly plunged into the water, killed the demon (Pañcajana) but could not trace the child in his bowels.

devanagari: तदङ्गप्रभवं शङ्खमादाय रथमागमत् । ततः संयमनीं नाम यमस्य दयितां पुरीम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaṅgaprabhavaṃ śaṅkhamādāya rathamāgamattataḥ saṃyamanīṃ nāma yamasya dayitāṃ purīm
translation: Taking the conch which formed his body, he returned to his chariot. He went with Balarāma to Saṃya- manī, the beloved city of Yama, the god of death and blew his conch. Hearing the loud blast of the conch, Yama, the chastiser of all beings, offered them worship on a grand scale and with deep devotion. Bowing down in humility, he submitted to Kṛṣṇa who abides in the hearts of all beings, “Oh Viṣṇu who have assumed a human form out of sport! What can I do for you both?”

devanagari: गत्वा जनार्दनः शङ्खं प्रदध्मौ सहलायुधः । शङ्खनिर्ह्रादमाकर्ण्य प्रजासंयमनो यमः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: gatvā janārdanaḥ śaṅkhaṃ pradadhmau sahalāyudhaḥśaṅkhanirhrādamākarṇya prajāsaṃyamano yamaḥ
translation: Taking the conch which formed his body, he returned to his chariot. He went with Balarāma to Saṃya- manī, the beloved city of Yama, the god of death and blew his conch. Hearing the loud blast of the conch, Yama, the chastiser of all beings, offered them worship on a grand scale and with deep devotion. Bowing down in humility, he submitted to Kṛṣṇa who abides in the hearts of all beings, “Oh Viṣṇu who have assumed a human form out of sport! What can I do for you both?”

devanagari: तयोः सपर्यां महतीं चक्रे भक्त्युपबृंहिताम् । उवाचावनतः कृष्णं सर्वभूताशयालयम् । लीलामनुष्ययोर्विष्णो युवयोः करवाम किम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ saparyāṃ mahatīṃ cakre bhaktyupabṛṃhitāmuvācāvanataḥ kṛṣṇaṃ sarvabhūtāśayālayamlīlāmanuṣyayorviṣṇo yuvayoḥ karavāma kim
translation: Taking the conch which formed his body, he returned to his chariot. He went with Balarāma to Saṃya- manī, the beloved city of Yama, the god of death and blew his conch. Hearing the loud blast of the conch, Yama, the chastiser of all beings, offered them worship on a grand scale and with deep devotion. Bowing down in humility, he submitted to Kṛṣṇa who abides in the hearts of all beings, “Oh Viṣṇu who have assumed a human form out of sport! What can I do for you both?”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । गुरुपुत्रमिहानीतं निजकर्मनिबन्धनम् । आनयस्व महाराज मच्छासनपुरस्कृतः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācaguruputramihānītaṃ nijakarmanibandhanamānayasva mahārāja macchāsanapuraskṛtaḥ
translation:
The glorious Lord replied:
Oh great Ruler! Even if the son of my preceptor is brought here due to the force of his Karma, bring him here in obedience to and under the sanction of my command.

devanagari: तथेति तेनोपानीतं गुरुपुत्रं यदूत्तमौ । दत्त्वा स्वगुरवे भूयो वृणीष्वेति तमूचतुः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti tenopānītaṃ guruputraṃ yadūttamaudattvā svagurave bhūyo vṛṇīṣveti tamūcatuḥ
translation: The preceptor’s son who, in compliance of his command, was brought back by the god of Death, was restored to their preceptor by those two eminent Yadus who requested him, “Be pleased to ask for any other boon.”

devanagari: श्रीगुरुरुवाच । सम्यक्सम्पादितो वत्स भवद्भ्यां गुरुनिष्क्रयः । को नु युष्मद्विधगुरोः कामानामवशिष्यते ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīgururuvācasamyaksampādito vatsa bhavadbhyāṃ guruniṣkrayaḥko nu yuṣmadvidhaguroḥ kāmānāmavaśiṣyate
translation:
The Preceptor (Sāndīpani) said:
Child Kṛṣṇa! The obligations of the preceptors have been richly and properly repaid by you both respectable pupils. What desire of your preceptor can remain unfulfilled?

devanagari: गच्छतं स्वगृहं वीरौ कीर्तिर्वामस्तु पावनी । छन्दांस्ययातयामानि भवन्त्विह परत्र च ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: gacchataṃ svagṛhaṃ vīrau kīrtirvāmastu pāvanīchandāṃsyayātayāmāni bhavantviha paratra ca
translation: Oh heroes! You return to your homes! May your glory be purifying to the world! May your memory and knowledge of the Vedas remain afresh here and hereafter.

devanagari: गुरुणैवमनुज्ञातौ रथेनानिलरंहसा । आयातौ स्वपुरं तात पर्जन्यनिनदेन वै ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: guruṇaivamanujñātau rathenānilaraṃhasāāyātau svapuraṃ tāta parjanyaninadena vai
translation: Oh child parīkṣit! Being thus permitted to return home, both of them returned to their capital in their chariot which had the speed of the wind and the thundering of the cloud.

devanagari: समनन्दन्प्रजाः सर्वा दृष्ट्वा रामजनार्दनौ । अपश्यन्त्यो बह्वहानि नष्टलब्धधना इव ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: samanandanprajāḥ sarvā dṛṣṭvā rāmajanārdanauapaśyantyo bahvahāni naṣṭalabdhadhanā iva
translation:
All the subjects who missed Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa for many days, were highly rejoiced to see them both, like persons who have recovered their lost wealth.
[back to top]
